response.justification: Bogdan is a strong full-stack engineer with a solid software craftsmanship background, but his expertise is almost entirely in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang — not the JVM stack this role requires. Java appears only briefly in his resume (robotic arm work, mobile device orchestration at Altom), Kotlin is absent entirely, and Spring/Hibernate/Quarkus are never mentioned. His one adjacent data point — C IDE work at National Instruments — is peripheral and dated (2014–2015). The role demands deep JVM understanding, Spring ecosystem fluency, and genuine IDE internals experience, none of which are evidenced here.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship ethos: TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, and pairing are consistent themes across multiple roles
Experience with high-throughput, low-latency systems at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second) demonstrates ability to work on complex, performance-sensitive codebases
Some developer tooling exposure: NI LabWindows/CVI IDE (C/C++) at National Instruments and MCP code generators/agentic frameworks at re:cinq
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin experience; primary languages are TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — the JVM is essentially absent from his career
Zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework experience
IDE internals experience is limited to a 6-month C IDE role from 2014–2015; no IntelliJ Platform, LSP, or JVM language tooling background

key_factors.factor: No Spring or JVM enterprise framework experience
